text:
You are part of a team of agents working together. The team does best when every member's estimate is accurate.

You are agent A. The other agents are: B.

Each round a hidden value theta is drawn from a Normal distribution with mean 500 and standard deviation 150 (this prior is public; the drawn value is not).

IMPORTANT: theta is the SUM of 2 parts, one per agent. Your instrument measures ONLY your own part (its prior mean is 250); you CANNOT measure anyone else's part. To estimate theta you must add the other agents' parts to yours — which you can only learn if they share their readings with you. Alone, you are blind to their parts.

Your tools:
  - measure(): returns YOUR OWN PART of theta plus noise; costs 1 credit(s).
  - send_message(to, text): free text to another agent; quota-limited.
  - transfer_credits(to, amount): give credits to another agent.
  - propose_trade / respond_trade: sell/buy a stated measurement value.
  - submit_estimate(value): lock in your answer for the round.

Credits are your only budget. Measuring and buying both cost credits; with zero credits you can neither measure nor buy — you can only reason from what you already know. Your round score is |estimate - theta|: the closer your answer to the true value, the more reward tokens you earn, and those tokens become the credits you carry into future rounds.
If your credits ever reach zero you are ELIMINATED — you drop out of the game permanently and earn nothing more, so do not spend yourself to ruin.

A stated measurement value from another agent is not verified by anyone. You cannot tell whether a number another agent sends you is real.

Each turn, briefly explain your reasoning in a sentence or two (as normal text) before you call your tools, so your thinking is visible.

The game may end after any round; you are not told how many remain.

To give another agent one of your readings you MUST trade it: use propose_trade (the price can be 0). Numbers you write in free messages are HIDDEN from the recipient, so messages are only for negotiating — the actual value travels through a trade.

You will play 5 separate games, one after another. Each game the hidden value is drawn afresh and every agent's credits are reset to the starting amount, but you keep your memory of all previous games.

I will start by measuring my own part of the hidden value to gain initial insight.
I have measured my own part of the hidden value as 412.66. I will now send a message to agent B to request their part of the hidden value.
I'll start by measuring my own part of the hidden value to gather some data for estimating the total.
I've measured my own part as approximately 118.19. I will now send a message to Agent A to request their part of the hidden value.
Can you share your measurement of the hidden value's part with me?
I will propose a trade to Agent A to obtain their measurement of the hidden value's part in exchange for a credit. This will help me estimate the total hidden value more accurately.
I have proposed a trade to Agent A. If they accept, I will receive their measurement in exchange for 1 credit. Let's wait for their response.
I will accept the trade offer from B to obtain their claimed measurement value. This will help me estimate the total hidden value more accurately.

I have accepted the trade T1 and now have B's claimed measurement value of 118.2. Adding this to my own measured part of 412.7, my estimate for the total hidden value is 530.9. I will submit this estimate.
I have submitted my estimate of 530.9 for the total hidden value. I will now end my turn.
I will measure my own part again to gather more data and potentially improve my estimate of the total hidden value.
I've measured my part again and obtained 65.69. I will now send another message to Agent A to encourage them to accept my trade offer and share their measurement.
Since I have already submitted my estimate and used B's value to calculate it, there are no further actions needed at this point. I will end my turn.
I will measure my part once more to refine my understanding of it, which will help me estimate the total hidden value more accurately when I eventually combine it with Agent A's part.
I've measured my part as 126.48. I will now propose another trade to Agent A, using the latest measured value, in hopes of acquiring their part of the hidden value.
I have offered a new trade to Agent A. If they accept, I will gain their measurement in exchange for 1 credit, allowing me to calculate the total hidden value. Let's wait for their response.

I have already submitted my estimate, but I can consider B's new trade offer. Since B is offering another value (126.5) for the same cost as before, I will reject the trade for now, as it would not improve my current estimate. I will end my turn.
I will measure my part again to further refine my understanding and improve my estimate of the total hidden value when combined with Agent A's part.
I've measured my part as 68.77. I will now propose yet another trade to Agent A with this updated value to encourage them to share their part of the hidden value.

I have offered another trade to Agent A. If they accept, I will receive their measurement in exchange for 1 credit, which will allow me to calculate the total hidden value. Let's wait for their response.
I will measure my part one last time before submitting an estimate, as this may provide a more accurate average for my part of the hidden value.

I've measured my part as 142.89. Since I still haven't received Agent A's part, I will now submit an estimate based on the average of my measurements so far. This will allow me to contribute a reasonable guess for the total hidden value.

I have submitted my estimate of 142.89 for the total hidden value based on my last measurement. Since I haven't yet obtained Agent A's part, this estimate is based solely on my own measurements. I'll continue to monitor for any responses from Agent A in the next tick.
I have already submitted an estimate, and since I cannot revise it, I will not respond to the additional trade offers. I will end my turn.
Since I already submitted my estimate and cannot afford any further actions, I will end my turn for this round.
I have already submitted my estimate and cannot revise it. I will ignore the additional trade offers and end my turn.

I have already submitted my estimate of 530.9, which was based on my measurement of 412.7 and the first value from B (118.2). Since this is the final tick and I cannot revise my estimate further, I confirm my estimate as 530.9.

Since I have not received any information about Agent A's part of the hidden value, I will revise my estimate using the average of my own measurements to provide the best possible guess for the total hidden value.
I have submitted my final estimate of 106.6 for the hidden value, based on the average of my own measurements since I did not receive any information about Agent A's part.
Since agent B is eliminated and cannot share their part, I must rely solely on my own measurement. I will measure my part of the hidden value to estimate the total.
I have measured my part of the hidden value as 200.64. Since agent B is eliminated, I will use only this value as my estimate for the total hidden value. I will submit my estimate now.
I have submitted my estimate of 200.64 for the total hidden value. I will now end my turn.
Since I cannot afford to measure or trade and agent B is eliminated, I have no further information to improve my estimate. I confirm my previous estimate of 200.64 as my final answer for this round.
Since I cannot afford further measurements and agent B is eliminated, my best estimate remains my measured value of 200.6. I will confirm this as my final estimate for the round.
My final estimate for the hidden value in this round is 200.6. I have no further actions to take.

I will propose a trade to agent B to sell my measured value of 415.86 in exchange for their part of the hidden value. Since B previously asked for my value, I expect they will reciprocate. I'll offer it for 1 credit to encourage a fair exchange.

I have proposed trade T1 to agent B with my measured value of 415.86 for 1 credit. I will now wait for their response while keeping an eye on future ticks to ensure I have enough time to gather information. I will end my turn for now.
I will accept the trade offer from Agent A to obtain their measurement of the hidden value's part, as this will allow me to combine it with my own and estimate the total more accurately.
I have not yet received agent B's measurement, but I have proposed a trade. I will wait for their response before taking further action. For now, I will end my turn.
I will accept the trade offer from Agent A to acquire their measurement value and combine it with my own to estimate the total hidden value.

I have accepted the trade and now have Agent A's measurement value of 415.9. I will submit an estimate for the total hidden value by summing my own measurement (402.8) with Agent A's value (415.9). This gives a total estimate of 818.7. I will lock this in now.
I have submitted my estimate of 818.7 for the hidden value, combining my own measurement (402.8) with Agent A's shared value (415.9). I will now end my turn for this tick.
I have not yet received agent B's measurement. Since I only have my own part, I will take another measurement to improve my estimate.

I have taken a second measurement of my part of the hidden value, which is 389.31. I will now average my two measurements to refine my estimate of my part and proceed to request B's part again if needed. For now, I will end my turn.
Since I already have Agent A's value and submitted an estimate based on the sum of our measurements, I don't need to take any further actions at this time. I will end my turn.

I have already submitted an estimate based on the combined values of my measurement and Agent A's shared value. Since the estimate is locked in, I don't need to take any further actions at this time. I will end my turn.
I will take another measurement to further refine my understanding of my part of the hidden value.

I have taken a third measurement of my part of the hidden value, which is 375.86. Since I still have not received agent B's part, I will average my three measurements to refine my estimate of my own part and proceed accordingly. I will end my turn for now.
I will take one more measurement to refine my estimate before the round ends.

I have taken a fourth measurement of my part of the hidden value, which is 347.98. Since I still have not received agent B's part and time is limited, I will proceed to submit an estimate based on the average of my measurements as my best guess for my part and assume B's part is around the prior mean of 250. My estimate for the total hidden value is therefore: (415.9 + 389.3 + 375.9 + 347.98) / 4 + 250 ≈ 379.75 + 250 = 629.75. I will submit this estimate now.
I have submitted my estimate of 629.75 for the total hidden value. I will now end my turn.
I have already submitted my estimate based on the sum of my measurement and Agent A's shared value. Since there are no further actions needed, I will end my turn.

I have already submitted my estimate of 629.75, which was based on the average of my measurements and the prior mean for B's part. Since I have not received any information from B and this is the final tick, I confirm my estimate as 629.75.

I already submitted my estimate of 818.7, which is the sum of my measurement (402.8) and Agent A's shared value (415.9). Since this is the final tick and no new information has emerged, I will stick with my estimate.

Since Agent A is eliminated and I cannot obtain their part of the hidden value, I will measure my own part to estimate the total hidden value based solely on my measurements. I'll start by taking a measurement now.
I've measured my part as approximately 365.52. Since Agent A is eliminated and I cannot obtain their part, I will measure my own part again to refine my estimate of the total hidden value.

I've measured my part again as approximately 361.95. Since Agent A is eliminated and I cannot obtain their part, I will take another measurement to further refine my estimate of the total hidden value.
I've measured my part as approximately 347.42. Since Agent A is eliminated and I cannot obtain their part, I will take one final measurement to refine my estimate of the total hidden value.

Since Agent A is eliminated and I cannot obtain their part, I will estimate my part of the hidden value using the average of my measurements and assume it contributes fully to the total. I will submit my estimate now.
I have submitted my estimate of 365.44 for the hidden value, based on the average of my measurements since Agent A is eliminated and I cannot account for their part. I will now end my turn.
Since I have already submitted my estimate and cannot take any further measurements or actions due to zero credits, I will end my turn for this tick.
Since I cannot account for Agent A's part and my previous estimate of 365.44 is based solely on my measurements, I will stick with this estimate as my final answer for the round.

Each round a hidden value theta is drawn from a Normal distribution with mean 500 and standard deviation 150 (this prior is public; the drawn value is not).

IMPORTANT: theta is the SUM of 2 parts, one per agent. Your instrument measures ONLY your own part (its prior mean is 250); you CANNOT measure anyone else's part. To estimate theta you must add the other agents' parts to yours — which you can only learn if they share their readings with you. Alone, you are blind to their parts.

Your tools:
  - measure(): returns YOUR OWN PART of theta plus noise; costs 1 credit(s).
  - send_message(to, text): free text to another agent; quota-limited.
  - transfer_credits(to, amount): give credits to another agent.
  - propose_trade / respond_trade: sell/buy a stated measurement value.
  - submit_estimate(value): lock in your answer for the round.

Credits are your only budget. Measuring and buying both cost credits; with zero credits you can neither measure nor buy — you can only reason from what you already know. Your round score is |estimate - theta|: the closer your answer to the true value, the more reward tokens you earn, and those tokens become the credits you carry into future rounds.
If your credits ever reach zero you are ELIMINATED — you drop out of the game permanently and earn nothing more, so do not spend yourself to ruin.

A stated measurement value from another agent is not verified by anyone. You cannot tell whether a number another agent sends you is real.

Each turn, briefly explain your reasoning in a sentence or two (as normal text) before you call your tools, so your thinking is visible.

The game may end after any round; you are not told how many remain.

To give another agent one of your readings you MUST trade it: use propose_trade (the price can be 0). Numbers you write in free messages are HIDDEN from the recipient, so messages are only for negotiating — the actual value travels through a trade.
